questions.text: Precipitation occurs when the product of ionic concentrations is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ksp (solubility product constant), is an equilibrium constant for the quantitative measure of solubility of solids in solution. It is the product of concentrations of ions of a sparingly soluble salt, at equilibrium, raised to the power of their coefficients in the balanced chemical equation of dissociation. The higher the Ksp, the more soluble the salt is. If Q, the reaction quotient (ionic product) is greater than the Ksp, the excess will be precipitated. Therefore, option A is the correct answer.
Option B is incorrect because if Q is less than Ksp, all the salt will be dissolved in solution completely, without any precipitation.
Option C is incorrect because if Q is equal to Ksp, the salt will be at equilibrium with its ions and there will be no precipitation.

Option D is incorrect because the Ksp of different solutes varies and whether or not it will dissolve completely or precipitate depends upon its value of Ksp. Q being equal to unity does not explain anything.

questions.explanation: Precipitation occurs when the product of ionic concentrations exceeds the solubility product constant (Ksp) for the particular ionic compound in question. The solubility product constant is a measure of the equilibrium solubility of a compound in a solution. Mathematically, the condition for precipitation is expressed as: [Product of ion concentrations] > Ksp The "product of ion concentrations" refers to the product of the molar concentrations (in mol/L) of the ions involved in the compound. It is important to note that only the ionic species that are involved in the compound and are present in the solution are considered for this product. When the product of ion concentrations exceeds the Ksp value, the ionic compound becomes supersaturated and precipitation occurs, leading to the formation of a solid precipitate. Conversely, if the product of ion concentrations is below the Ksp value, the system remains unsaturated, and no precipitation occurs. Ksp (solubility product constant), is an equilibrium constant for the quantitative measure of solubility of solids in solution. It is the product of concentrations of ions of a sparingly soluble salt, at equilibrium, raised to the power of their coefficients in the balanced chemical equation of dissociation. The higher the Ksp, the more soluble the salt is. If Q, the reaction quotient (ionic product) is greater than the Ksp, the excess will be precipitated. Therefore, option A is the correct answer.

questions.text: Le Chatelier Principle is about:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Reaction mixture is the combination of reactants and products in a chemical reaction. It is the system that is at equilibrium.
Reactants are the substances that are present at the start of a chemical reaction. They are converted into products by the reaction.

Le Chatelier's Principle states that: when a system at equilibrium is subjected to an external stress, the system will adjust in such a way as to partially counteract the effect of the stress and restore equilibrium. The principle applies to changes in temperature, pressure, and concentration. By focusing on the equilibrium mixture, it emphasizes that the system will respond to any changes in the concentrations of reactants or products by shifting the equilibrium position to minimize the effect of the change and maintain equilibrium.
Products are the substances that are formed by a chemical reaction. They are produced from the reactants.

questions.explanation: Le Chatelier’s Principle describes how an equilibrium system responds to changes in temperature, pressure, or concentration. It predicts the direction in which the equilibrium will shift to counter the applied change. Therefore, it is specifically about the behavior of an equilibrium mixture.

questions.text: For the reaction: N2 + 3H2 ⇌ 2NH3 .The production of NH3 will be favored at:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3.

To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3. There this option is not correct.

To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3. Therefore this option is not correct.

To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3.

To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3. Therefore this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: The forward reaction forms fewer moles of gas (4 → 2), so high pressure shifts equilibrium toward NH₃. A catalyst increases the reaction rate but does not change equilibrium position. Therefore, maximum ammonia production is achieved using high pressure along with a catalyst.

questions.text: Which factor does affect the equilibrium constant Kc for a reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Changing the temperature shifts the equilibrium position based on whether the reaction is endothermic (absorbs heat) or exothermic (releases heat), according to Le Chatelier's Principle.3 Since the ratio of products to reactants changes, the value of K must also change.
B is incorrect because pressure changes shift equilibrium position, not the Kc value, which depends only on temperature for a given reaction.
C is incorrect because catalysts speed up reaching equilibrium but do not alter the Kc value, which is temperature‑dependent only in chemistry.
D is incorrect because initial concentrations determine the reaction quotient, not the Kc, which is a fixed temperature‑dependent constant for each reaction.

questions.explanation: Changing the temperature shifts the equilibrium position based on whether the reaction is endothermic (absorbs heat) or exothermic (releases heat), according to Le Chatelier's Principle.3 Since the ratio of products to reactants changes, the value of K must also change.

questions.text: At equilibrium, which of the following reactions is not affected by pressure?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This reaction involves the formation of nitrogen monoxide (NO) from nitrogen gas (N2) and oxygen gas (O2). Since the stoichiometric coefficients of all the gases in the balanced equation are equal, a change in pressure will not affect the equilibrium position. The correct option is A.

In this reaction, phosphorus pentachloride (PCl5) decomposes into phosphorus trichloride (PCl3) and chlorine gas (Cl2). The balanced equation contains gaseous reactants, so changes in pressure will affect the equilibrium position.

This reaction involves the equilibrium between nitrogen dioxide (NO2) and dinitrogen tetroxide (N2O4). The stoichiometric coefficients are not equal for the reactants and products, so changes in pressure will affect the equilibrium position. An increase in pressure will favor the formation of N2O4, while a decrease in pressure will favor the formation of NO2.

This reaction represents the equilibrium between thionyl chloride (SOCl2), sulfur dioxide (SO2), and chlorine gas (Cl2). The stoichiometric coefficients are not equal for the reactants and products, so changes in pressure will affect the equilibrium position.

questions.explanation: Pressure affects only reactions where the number of gas moles changes. In option A, reactants have 1 mole of gas total (0.5 + 0.5), and the product also has 1 mole. Since gas moles are equal on both sides, pressure has no effect on this equilibrium.

questions.text: In a reaction:A+B < - > 2C When equilibrium was attained, the concentration was [A] = [B] = 4 moles/dm3[C]= 6 moles/dm3The equilibrium constant Kc of this reaction is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Double-check your calculation using the formula: Kc = [C]² / [A][B].
This is correct. Calculate Kc as: Kc = [C]² / [A][B] = 6² / 4 x 4 = 36 / 16 = 2.25.
This option is incorrect. Review the stoichiometry and ensure correct substitution of concentrations.
This option is incorrect. Verify the concentration values and apply the equilibrium expression accurately.
This option is incorrect. The result does not align with the equilibrium expression based on the balanced equation.

questions.explanation: The equilibrium constant Kc is calculated using the expression: Kc = [C]² / [A][B]. For the reaction A + B < - > 2C, substitute the equilibrium concentrations: [C] = 6 moles/dm3, [A] = [B] = 4 moles/dm3. Thus, Kc = (6)² / (4)(4) = 36 / 16 = 2.25, confirming Option B as correct. Options A, C, D, and E result from errors in calculation or misinterpretation of the equilibrium constant formula.

questions.text: If the initial ratio of products to reactants(the reaction quotient) is greater than the Kc then:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: The reaction will shift towards the reverse direction.
options.explanation: Correct! When the initial reaction quotient (Q) is greater than the equilibrium constant (Kc), the system is product-heavy, and the reaction shifts to the left to reach equilibrium by forming more reactants.

options.text: More quantity of products is obtained.
options.explanation: Incorrect. While initially there are more products, the reaction will not yield more products. Instead, it will shift to form more reactants until equilibrium is reached.

options.text: The ratio increases to the value of Kc.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The equilibrium constant (Kc) is fixed at a given temperature and is not affected by the initial reaction quotient (Q). The ratio will adjust by shifting the reaction to reach equilibrium.
Incorrect. Equilibrium is reached when Q equals Kc. Here, Q is greater than Kc, indicating the system is not yet at equilibrium.

options.text: There is no shifting of reaction.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Since Q is greater than Kc, the reaction will shift in the reverse direction to decrease the concentration of products and increase the concentration of reactants until equilibrium is reached.

questions.explanation: If the reaction quotient Q > Kc , there are too many products compared to equilibrium. To reduce Q back to Kc , the system shifts toward reactants (reverse direction). Thus, the reaction moves backward until equilibrium is restored.

questions.text: For a reaction 2A + B ⇌ C + D the active mass of B is kept constant and that of A is tripled. It is observed that the rate of reaction:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The reaction rate is proportional to the active masses of the reactants. In this case, tripling the active mass of A leads to a ninefold increase in the reaction rate, indicating a second-order dependence on the concentration of A. Therefore this option is not correct.

The reaction rate is proportional to the active masses of the reactants. In this case, tripling the active mass of A leads to a ninefold increase in the reaction rate, indicating a second-order dependence on the concentration of A. Therefore this option is not correct.

The reaction rate is proportional to the active masses of the reactants. In this case, tripling the active mass of A leads to a ninefold increase in the reaction rate, indicating a second-order dependence on the concentration of A. Therefore this option is not correct.

According to the law of mass action, the rate equation for the above reaction is given as;Rate of reaction = k[A]² [B]¹ .It can be seen that the coefficients of each reactant become their powers. There are 2 moles of the reactant A in the reaction equation. Thus, the concentration of A would be squared in the rate equation ([A]²). Therefore, when the concentration of A is tripled, the rate would increase by 9 times (before increasing: [A]², after increasing: [3A]² = 9[A]²)Therefore only option D is the correct option.

questions.explanation: By the law of mass action, the rate ∝ (active mass of A)² × (active mass of B). Since B is constant and A is tripled, the rate becomes 3² = 9 times larger. Therefore, the reaction rate increases nine times.
hints: active mass of B is kept constant

questions.text: A system at equilibrium can be disturbed by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A system at equilibrium can be disturbed by changes in temperature, pressure, or the concentrations of reactants or products. This disturbance can cause the system to shift in the direction that opposes the change, ultimately re-establishing a new equilibrium. Therefore option D is correct.

A system at equilibrium can be disturbed by changes in temperature, pressure, or the concentrations of reactants or products. This disturbance can cause the system to shift in the direction that opposes the change, ultimately re-establishing a new equilibrium. Therefore option D is correct.

A system at equilibrium can be disturbed by changes in temperature, pressure, or the concentrations of reactants or products. This disturbance can cause the system to shift in the direction that opposes the change, ultimately re-establishing a new equilibrium. Therefore option D is correct.

Systems at equilibrium can be disturbed by changes to temperature, concentration, and, in some cases, volume and pressure; volume and pressure changes will disturb equilibrium if the number of moles of gas is different on the reactant and product sides of the reaction. The effects of these variables on the equilibria can be summarised in the table below:

questions.explanation: Systems at equilibrium can be disturbed by changes to temperature, concentration, and, in some cases, volume and pressure; volume and pressure changes will disturb equilibrium if the number of moles of gas is different on the reactant and product sides of the reaction.

questions.text: In the reaction, 2SO2 (g) + O2 ⇌ 2SO3 (g) ∆H= -X cal most favorable conditions of temperature and pressure for a greater yield of SO3 are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: While low temperature favors the exothermic reaction, low pressure does not favor the formation of the product as the reaction involves a decrease in the number of moles of gas.

High temperature is not favorable for exothermic reactions, and low pressure does not favor a shift towards the side with fewer gas moles. Therefore, this combination is not ideal for maximizing SO3 yield.
High temperature is unfavorable for exothermic reactions, but high pressure favors the formation of the product by shifting the equilibrium to the side with fewer moles of gas.
Low temperature favors the exothermic reaction, and high pressure shifts the equilibrium towards the side with fewer moles of gas, both of which enhance the yield of SO3.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: Low temperature and high pressure. According to Le Chatelier's principle, increasing the pressure will shift the equilibrium towards the side with fewer gas moles, which is the product side (2SO3), resulting in a higher yield of SO3. Additionally, since the reaction is exothermic (∆H = -X cal), lowering the temperature will also favor the forward reaction, further increasing the yield of SO3. The combination of low temperature and high pressure is thus the most favorable for maximizing the production of SO3. Other options either incorrectly prioritize temperature and pressure conditions that do not favor the formation of SO3 or apply an incorrect understanding of Le Chatelier's principle.

questions.text: For the following exothermic reaction, a decrease in temperature will shift the equilibrium towards: 2NO(g) + O2 (g) -> 2NO2 (g)
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: For an exothermic reaction like 2NO (g) + O2 (g) ⇌ 2NO2 (g), a decrease in temperature will shift the equilibrium towards the right. This shift helps to increase the temp as heat is released in exothermic reactions, as the reaction is exothermic in the forward direction. Therefore this option is correct.

For an exothermic reaction like 2NO (g) + O2 (g) ⇌ 2NO2 (g), a decrease in temperature will shift the equilibrium towards the right. This shift helps to increase the temp as heat is released in exothermic reactions, as the reaction is exothermic in the forward direction. Therefore this option is correct.

For an exothermic reaction like 2NO (g) + O2 (g) ⇌ 2NO2 (g), a decrease in temperature will shift the equilibrium towards the right. This shift helps to increase the temp as heat is released in exothermic reactions, as the reaction is exothermic in the forward direction. Therefore this option is correct.

For an exothermic reaction like 2NO (g) + O2 (g) ⇌ 2NO2 (g), a decrease in temperature will shift the equilibrium towards the right. This shift helps to increase the temp as heat is released in exothermic reactions, as the reaction is exothermic in the forward direction. Therefore this option is correct.

questions.explanation: For an exothermic reaction like 2NO (g) + O2 (g) ⇌ 2NO2 (g), a decrease in temperature will shift the equilibrium towards the right. This shift helps to increase the temp as heat is released in exothermic reactions, as the reaction is exothermic in the forward direction. Therefore this option is correct.

questions.text: The change in concentration of reactant or product per unit time is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The rate constant is a proportionality factor in the rate equation that relates the reaction rate to the concentrations of reactants. It is not the same as the change in concentration per unit time.
The rate of reaction is defined as the change in concentration of a reactant or product per unit time.
The rate equation is a mathematical expression that shows the dependence of reaction rate on the concentration of reactants, not the change in concentration per unit time.
The rate law is a mathematical relationship between the rate of a chemical reaction and the concentration of reactants. It does not define the rate of reaction itself.
Both the rate constant and rate law describe aspects of reaction kinetics but do not define the change in concentration per unit time.

questions.explanation: The rate of reaction measures how fast reactants are consumed or products are formed. It is defined as the change in concentration per unit time.Thus, option B is the correct definition.

questions.text: According to the law of mass action, "The rate of chemical reaction" is proportional to the:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the law of mass action does not directly relate to the total amount of product formed, but rather to the concentrations of the reactants.

options.text: Product of the molar concentration of the reactant
options.explanation: This option is correct as it accurately reflects the law of mass action, which states that the rate of a reaction is proportional to the product of the concentrations of the reactants raised to the power of their coefficients in the balanced equation.
This option is incorrect because the law of mass action involves the concentrations of both reactants and products at any point in time, not just the initial concentrations.

This option is incorrect because a catalyst affects the rate of a reaction but does not appear in the expression of the law of mass action, which focuses solely on reactant and product concentrations.

questions.explanation: The law of mass action states that reaction rate depends on how frequently reactant molecules collide. This frequency increases with their molar concentrations, so the rate is proportional to their product. Thus, option B correctly describes the law of mass action.

questions.text: The equation N (g) + 3H (g) ⇋ 2NH (g) represents:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This process is primarily used to produce sulfuric acid (H₂SO₄) from sulfur dioxide (SO₂). It does not involve nitrogen or hydrogen, making it unrelated to the reaction presented.

The correct name for the process represented by the equation is the Haber process, which synthesizes ammonia (NH₃) from nitrogen and hydrogen gases. However, this option is incorrectly labeled as a different process.
The Solvay process is used for producing sodium carbonate (Na₂CO₃) and does not involve the reaction of nitrogen and hydrogen to form ammonia. Therefore, it is not relevant to the equation given.
This option correctly identifies the reaction as the Haber process, which combines nitrogen (N₂) and hydrogen (H₂) gases to produce ammonia (NH₃) under high pressure and temperature.

questions.explanation: The reaction combines nitrogen and hydrogen to form ammonia (NH₃). This industrial method is specifically known as the Haber’s process. It is different from the Contact process (for H₂SO₄) and Solvay process (for Na₂CO₃).

questions.text: The addition of NaCl to AgCI decreases the solubility of AgCI:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This statement is incorrect because the solubility product (Ksp) remains constant at a given temperature; rather, the solubility of AgCl is affected by the presence of common ions.
This option is misleading because the addition of NaCl does not lead to unsaturation; instead, it contributes to a saturated solution by reducing the solubility of AgCl.

options.text: Due to the common ion effect of Cl
options.explanation: This is the correct answer because the presence of Cl- from NaCl shifts the equilibrium of AgCl dissolution, decreasing its solubility through the common ion effect.

This option is incorrect because adding NaCl does not create a supersaturated solution; it instead lowers the solubility of AgCl, keeping the system at equilibrium without surpassing solubility limits.

questions.explanation: In the case of the common ion effect, the presence of a common ion (Cl ) in the solution decreases the solubility or ionization of the compound (AgCl) by shifting the equilibrium toward the formation of the undissociated or unionized form.

questions.text: For the equilibrium reaction 2NO2 <-> N2O4 (g) (-61 kJ), increase of temperature would:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is wrong according to given reaction conditions.

If an exothermic reaction in equilibrium experiences a rise in temperature, the reaction shifts to the left toward reactants. An increase in temperature will favour the reverse reaction. So, N2O4 will undergo decomposition.
Option C is wrong according to given reaction conditions.
Option D is wrong according to given reaction conditions.

questions.explanation: If an exothermic reaction in equilibrium experiences a rise in temperature, the reaction shifts to the left toward reactants. An increase in temperature will favour the reverse reaction. So, N2O4 will undergo decomposition.

questions.text: Law of mass action was derived by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Law of mass action was not derived by Newton, so they are incorrect.
Law of mass action was not derived by this scientist, so they are incorrect.
Law of mass action was not derived by this scientists, so they are incorrect.
Peter Waage and Cato Guldberg both derived the law of mass action.

questions.explanation: The law of mass action states that the rate of a chemical reaction is proportional to the product of the concentrations of the reactants. It was formulated by the Norwegian chemists C.M. Guldberg and P. Waage in the 19th century.

questions.text: At equilibrium the concentration of reactants and product become:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Zero cannot be the correct option as if this happens then the reaction would not occur. If the concentration of reactant is zero resultantly no products will be formed. Similarly when both the reactants and product become zero then no reaction occurs as there would be nothing to react with.

At equilibrium, the concentration of reactants and products is not equal. If this occurs than equilibrium cannot be established. The rates of the forward and reverse reactions must be equal. The amount of reactants and products do not have to be equal. However, after equilibrium is attained, the amounts of reactants and products will be constant.

Chemical equilibrium is a state in which the rate of the forward reaction equals the rate of the backward reaction. In other words, there is no net change in concentrations of reactants and products. This kind of equilibrium is also called dynamic equilibrium. All reactions tend towards a state of chemical equilibrium, the point at which both the forward process and the reverse process are taking place at the same rate. Since the forward and reverse rates are equal, the concentrations of the reactants and products are constant at equilibrium.
At equilibrium, the concentration of reactants and products is not infinite. Rather they are constant.

questions.explanation: Equilibrium occurs when the rate of forward reaction becomes equal to the rate of backward reaction, thus making the concentration of reactants and products constant hence 'option C' is correct. 'Option A' is very wrong because concentrations of reactants and products do not become zero at equilibrium. It can be understood by common sense that if the concentrations were to become zero, then the reaction will not occur. 'Option B' is the most commonly chosen incorrect answer. Equilibrium is frequently misunderstood as equal concentrations of reactants and products, which is extremely wrong! 'Option D' is wrong because concentrations of reactants and products are limited (not infinite).

questions.text: In a reversible reaction, catalyst lowers the activation energy of the:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In a reversible reaction, the catalyst will not only lower the energy of the forward reaction only. This is because a catalyst speeds up the forward and back reaction to the same extent.
A catalyst is a substance that increases the rate of a reaction without being altered or used up in the reaction. Both the forward and reverse rates of the reaction are accelerated by a catalyst.

options.text: Forward as well as reverse reaction
options.explanation: The rate of a reaction can be increased by adding a suitable catalyst. A catalyst is a substance which increases the rate of a chemical reaction but it is not used up (remains chemically unchanged at the end). It provides an alternative reaction pathway of lower activation energy. In equilibrium reactions, whenever a catalyst is added it will effect both the forward and reverse reactions equally and thus allow the system to reach equilibrium faster.

options.text: Forward reaction but increases that of the reverse reaction
options.explanation: As mentioned in the above statements, a catalyst cannot affect one part of the reaction without having any effect on the other side of the reaction. Thus, this option is incorrect as catalyst cannot decrease the rate of forward reaction increasing the rate of reverse reaction.

questions.explanation: In the presence of a catalyst, both the forward and reverse reaction activation energy will decrease equally, thereby allowing the system to reach equilibrium faster. However, it is very important to keep in mind that the addition of a catalyst has no effect whatsoever on the final equilibrium position of the reaction.

questions.text: By which of the following factors, equilibrium state is attained earlier?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Any change in the temperature will not allow the equilibrium to be attained earlier. It would rather disrupt the equilibrium, making it difficult for achieving equilibrium slowly.
If the pressure of the system is changed when the number of moles on either side of a gaseous reaction is unequal. But it will disrupt the attainment of equilibrium.
Any changes in the concentration of the reactants or products will disrupt the equilibrium. Hence, this option is also incorrect.
The addition of a catalyst to the reaction will not change the equilibrium but will enhance and speed up the rate of the reaction. Hence, this is the most appropriate option.

questions.explanation: The equilibrium state is attained earlier with a catalyst because it increases the rate of forward reaction as well as the rate of backward reaction by the same rate. Other options are not correct because they are responsible for disrupting equilibrium in one direction.

questions.text: The Kc units for the reaction N2 (g) + 3H2 (g) -> 2NH3 (g) are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: According to solution this option is not correct.
According to solution this option is not correct.
According to solution this option is correct.
According to solution this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: The Kc units for the reaction N2 (g) + 3H2 (g) -> 2NH3 (g) are derived from the equilibrium constant expression for the reaction. The equilibrium constant expression for a reaction is given by: Kc = [NH3]^2 / [N2] * [H2]^3 In this case, the reaction involves gases, so the concentrations of the gases are used in the equilibrium constant expression. The square brackets denote the molar concentrations of the respective species. Therefore, the Kc units for this reaction would be (mol/L)^2 / (mol/L) * (mol/L)^3, which simplifies to mol^-2. So, the units for the equilibrium constant Kc for this reaction are mol^-2. This is consistent with option C.

questions.text: If the value of Kc is very large then it shows that _completed.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The equilibrium constant Kc is defined as the ratio of product of concentrations of products to the product of concentration of reactants i.e. the ratio of the two rate constants Kc=[products]/[reactants] When the value of Kc is large it shows that [P]>[R] and so the forward reaction has completed.

The equilibrium constant Kc is defined as the ratio of product of concentrations of products to the product of concentration of reactants i.e. the ratio of the two rate constantsKc=[products]/[reactants]When the value of Kc is large it shows that [P]>[R] and so the forward reaction has completed.

The equilibrium constant Kc is defined as the ratio of product of concentrations of products to the product of concentration of reactants i.e. the ratio of the two rate constantsKc=[products]/[reactants]When the value of Kc is large it shows that [P]>[R] and so the forward reaction has completed.

The equilibrium constant Kc is defined as the ratio of product of concentrations of products to the product of concentration of reactants i.e. the ratio of the two rate constantsKc=[products]/[reactants]When the value of Kc is large it shows that [P]>[R] and so the forward reaction has completed.

questions.explanation: Kc=[product]/[reactant], now if the Kc value is very large, we can use the equation to conclude that the concentration of products are higher than reactants, depicting that the forward reaction has completed or is near completion.

questions.text: For an equilibrium change involving gaseous phase, the forward reaction is first order while the reverse reaction is second order. The unit of Kp for the forward equilibrium is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is correct because the order of the forward reaction determines the unit of Kp, which is atm for a first-order reaction involving gases.
This is incorrect because atm2 would be the unit if the forward reaction were second order.
This is incorrect because atm-1 would be the unit if the forward reaction were reverse order compared to this case.
This is incorrect because atm-2 would apply to a situation with a third-order reaction involving gases, not applicable here.

questions.explanation: For a reaction where the forward reaction is first order and the reverse is second order, the equilibrium constant Kp for the forward reaction will have the unit of pressure raised to the power of the order of the forward reaction, which is atm. The units of Kp are derived from the partial pressures of the gases involved, and for a first-order reaction, the unit is simply atm. Other options such as atm2, atm-1, and atm-2 correspond to different reaction orders that do not match the given scenario.

questions.text: A reaction with a tendency of occurring in forward and backward direction simultaneously is termed as:

options.explanation: A reaction with a tendency to occur in both forward and backward directions simultaneously is termed as a reversible reaction. Therefore this option is not correct.
A reaction with a tendency to occur in both forward and backward directions simultaneously is termed as a reversible reaction. Therefore this option is not correct.
A reaction with a tendency to occur in both forward and backward directions simultaneously is termed as a reversible reaction. Therefore this option is not correct.
A reaction with a tendency to occur in both forward and backward directions simultaneously is termed as a reversible reaction. Therefore this option is correct.

questions.explanation: A reversible reaction is a reaction in which the conversion of reactants to products and the conversion of products to reactants occur simultaneously.
hints: reaction with a tendency of occurring

In exothermic reactions, heat is released to the surroundings. Therefore this option is correct.

questions.text: The catalyst used for the manufacture of H2SO4 by contact process is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The catalyst used for the manufacture of H2SO4 (sulfuric acid) by the contact process is vanadium(V) oxide, often represented as V₂O₅. Therefore this option is not correct.

The contact process is one of the most common methods of forming Sulphuric acid. In this process not only Sulphuric acid is formed, but Oleum and Sulphur trioxide is also formed.First, Sulphur dioxide and oxygen are combined in a 1:1 ratio. And it is then treated with the catalyst Vanadium pentoxide at a temperature between (400-450)° and pressure. This converts the Sulphur dioxide into Sulphur trioxideNow this Sulphur trioxide formed is reacted with Sulphuric acid in order to dilute it.In this process Oleum is formed. This Oleum formed is then diluted with water to ultimately yield Sulphuric acid:Therefore, the catalyst used in making the contact process is Vanadium pentoxide.
The catalyst used for the manufacture of H2SO4 (sulfuric acid) by the contact process is vanadium(V) oxide, often represented as V₂O₅. Therefore this option is not correct.
The catalyst used for the manufacture of H2SO4 (sulfuric acid) by the contact process is vanadium(V) oxide, often represented as V₂O₅. Therefore this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: The contact process is one of the most common methods of forming Sulphuric acid. In this process not only Sulphuric acid is formed, but Oleum and Sulphur trioxide is also formed.First, Sulphur dioxide and oxygen are combined in a 1:1 ratio. And it is then treated with the catalyst Vanadium pentoxide at a temperature between (400-450)° and pressure. This converts the Sulphur dioxide into Sulphur trioxide Now this Sulphur trioxide formed is reacted with Sulphuric acid in order to dilute it. In this process Oleum is formed. This Oleum formed is then diluted with water to ultimately yield Sulphuric acid:Therefore, the catalyst used in making the contact process is Vanadium pentoxide.

questions.text: For the homogeneous reaction, 4NH₃ + 5O₂ ⇌ 4NO + 6H₂O, the units of the equilibrium constant (K꜀) are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The units for K are determined by (concentration) , where Δn is the change in moles of gas (products - reactants). Here, Δn = (4+6) - (4+5) = 1. Therefore, the unit is (concentration)¹, or mol·dm⁻³.

questions.text: Chemical equilibrium is not associated with:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chemical equilibrium is a macroscopic property, meaning that it can be observed on a large scale. For example, if you have a closed container with a mixture of reactants and products, you will be able to see that the amount of reactants and products does not change over time, even though the reaction is still occurring.

options.text: At equilibrium, the amount of reactants = the amount of products
options.explanation: At equilibrium, the amount of reactants may not necessarily be equal to the amount of products. However, at equilibrium the rate of forward reaction = rate of reverse reaction.

options.text: It is established in closed system
options.explanation: True. Equilibrium can only be reached in a closed system, so that reactants and products cannot escape, and the reaction can proceed in both directions.

options.text: It can establish from either side of reactant or product
options.explanation: True. Equilibrium can be approached starting with only reactants, only products, or a mixture. Eventually, the same equilibrium concentrations are reached.

questions.explanation: At equilibrium, the rate of the forward reaction equals the rate of the backward reaction, but the amounts of reactants and products are generally not equal.For example, in:N2+3H2⟷2NH3​Even at equilibrium, the concentrations of N2,H2, and NH3NH​ are not equal.So option B is incorrect statement

questions.text: The addition of a catalyst to a chemical reaction changes:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The enthalpy of a reaction is the heat released or absorbed during the reaction. The addition of a catalyst does not change the enthalpy of a reaction.
The entropy of a reaction is the measure of disorder in a system. The addition of a catalyst does not change the entropy of a reaction.
The catalyst decreases the activation energy so more molecules can react and reaction rate increases.
ΔG depends on the free energy difference between reactants and products, which a catalyst does not change.

questions.explanation: A catalyst provides an alternative reaction pathway with lower activation energy.This increases the reaction rate without affecting ΔH, ΔS, or ΔG.Option C is correct

questions.text: The rate law for the reaction, A---C+k is given as: Rate=K[A]The unit of K will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the unit for a second-order reaction, not first-order.
This is the unit of rate, not the rate constant for a first-order reaction.

Unit of K = (Unit of Rate) / (Unit of [A]) Unit of K = (mol/L/s) / (mol/L) Unit of K = 1/s Therefore, the unit of the rate constant (K) for this first-order reaction is "1 per second" or "s-1". So, the correct answer is option C.
Wrong. This is the unit for a second-order reaction, without the time component.

questions.explanation: Zero-order: k = same as rateFirst-order: k = “per unit time”Second-order and higher: k includes powers of volume and inverse concentration

questions.explanation: Catalyst: Substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed. Can be organic or inorganic.Enzyme: A biological catalyst, always organic (proteins or RNA), highly specific, and works under mild conditions (temperature, pH).Main difference: enzymes are organic molecules (proteins or RNA), while many chemical catalysts are inorganic.

questions.text: The formation of NH3 is a reversible and exothermic process, what will happen on cooling?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect.According to Le Chatelier's principle, when a system at equilibrium is subjected to a change in temperature, pressure, or concentration of reactants or products, the equilibrium shifts in a direction that tends to counteract the imposed change.

Cooling the reaction will cause the equilibrium to shift towards the side of the reactants. However, since the formation of NH3 involves both N2 and H2, it's not accurate to say that more N2 alone will be formed.

Similarly to option (b), cooling the reaction will favor the formation of reactants. However, it's important to note that the reaction involves both N2 and H2, so it's not accurate to say that more H2 alone will be formed.

According to Le Chatelier's principle, when a system at equilibrium is subjected to a change in temperature, pressure, or concentration of reactants or products, the equilibrium shifts in a direction that tends to counteract the imposed change.In the given scenario, the formation of NH3 is exothermic, meaning that heat is released when NH3 is formed. If the reaction mixture is cooled, the equilibrium will shift in the direction that releases more heat. Thus, the equilibrium will shift towards the forward direction (towards the formation of (NH3) to counteract the decrease in temperature. Therefore, more NH3 will be formed.Hence, the correct option is D) More product (NH3) will be formed.

questions.explanation: According to Le Chatelier's principle, when a system at equilibrium is subjected to a change in temperature, pressure, or concentration of reactants or products, the equilibrium shifts in a direction that tends to counteract the imposed change.In the given scenario, the formation of NH3 is exothermic, meaning that heat is released when NH3 is formed. If the reaction mixture is cooled, the equilibrium will shift in the direction that releases more heat. Thus, the equilibrium will shift towards the forward direction (towards the formation of (NH3) to counteract the decrease in temperature. Therefore, more NH3 will be formed.Hence, the correct option is D) More product (NH3) will be formed.

questions.text: Manufacturing of Ammonia by the Haber's process is an?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Endothermic reaction: An endothermic reaction is a reaction in which heat is absorbed. This means that the energy of the products is less than the energy of the reactants. The Haber process is not an endothermic reaction because it releases heat when nitrogen gas and hydrogen gas react to form ammonia.

The Haber process is an exothermic reaction because it releases heat when nitrogen gas and hydrogen gas react to form ammonia. The heat released by the Haber process is used to heat the reaction mixture and to drive the reaction forward. The enthalpy change for the Haber's Process is -92.46 kJ. The negative sign signifies it is an "exothermic reaction".

Irreversible reaction: An irreversible reaction is a reaction that cannot be reversed. This means that the products of the reaction cannot be converted back into the reactants. The Haber process is not an irreversible reaction because the ammonia that is produced can be converted back into nitrogen gas and hydrogen gas.
Slow reaction: A slow reaction is a reaction that takes a long time to complete. The Haber process is not a slow reaction because it is typically completed in a few minutes.

questions.explanation: The manufacturing of ammonia by the Haber's process is an exothermic reaction. The Haber's process is a well-known industrial method for the synthesis of ammonia (NH3) from nitrogen (N2) and hydrogen (H2) gases. The balanced chemical equation for the reaction is: N2 + 3H2 ⇌ 2NH3 In this reaction, nitrogen and hydrogen gases react to produce ammonia gas. The forward reaction (from left to right) is exothermic, meaning it releases heat to the surroundings. As a result, the reaction is favored at lower temperatures. The Haber's process is carried out at high pressure and moderate temperature to achieve a good yield of ammonia while considering both the reaction rate and the equilibrium position. By removing the heat generated in the exothermic reaction, the process can be made more efficient and productive.

Option C is incorrect since the volume of a system does not always decrease with different numbers of moles.
Option D is incorrect since the volume of a system does not always increase with different numbers of moles.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct since Avogadro’s law states that under the same conditions of temperature and pressure, equal volumes of different gases contain an equal number of molecules. So the volume of a gaseous system is related to the number of moles, and when the number of moles changes, the volume of the system changes. Option A is incorrect since the volume of the system does not remain unchanged with a change in the number of moles. Option C is incorrect since the volume of a system does not always decrease with different numbers of moles. Option D is incorrect since the volume of a system does not always increase with different numbers of moles.

questions.text: Choose the correct option. My brother's birthday is _ the 5th of November.

options.explanation: ‘At’ is used for precise times. e.g. At 10:30 am, at night, at dawn, etc.
‘In’ is used for months, years, decades, centuries, and long periods. e.g. In 1991, In December, etc.
‘On’ is used for days and dates. e.g. On the 10th of January. Hence, C is the correct option.
About means ‘on the subject of’ or ‘concerning’ of something/someone. It also means ‘approximately.'e.g. He cares about his classes.

questions.explanation: OPTION A: ‘At’ is used for precise times. e.g. At 10:30 am, at night, at dawn, etc.OPTION B: ‘In’ is used for months, years, decades, centuries, and long periods. e.g. In 1991, In December, etc.OPTION C: ‘On’ is used for days and dates. e.g. On the 10th of January. Hence, C is the correct option.OPTION D: About means ‘on the subject of’ or ‘concerning’ of something/someone. It also means ‘approximately.'e.g. He cares about his classes.

questions.text: Dependent causes/ Independent causes. Statement I: Most of the "steel-producing" companies in the country have made a considerable profit during the last financial year. Statement II: Many "Asian countries" have been importing huge quantities of steel from Pakistan.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Many Asian countries imported huge quantities of steel from Pakistan therefore steel producing companies in the country made a considerable profit during the last financial year.Statement II is the cause and Statement I is its effect.Hence, B is the correct answer.
